content.content:
1	name: Build Windows ARM64 (vendored fork)
2	
3	# Deterministic, NO-AI build of the vendored fork source at repo root.
4	# Unlike the legacy pipeline there is NO upstream clone, NO patch apply, NO AI
5	# re-anchor and NO arch-fixup: every Windows/ARM64 change already lives in the
6	# committed source (the fork tree). This workflow only: install -> build ->
7	# materialize the win-arm64 native closure -> package the NSIS installer ->
8	# verify the packaged natives are ARM64 -> (optionally) publish a release.
9	#
10	# It is the build half of the architecture: the baseline is frozen in this repo;
11	# nightly-merge.yml carries upstream deltas in and calls a build like this to
12	# prove the result before publishing.
13	
14	on:
15	  workflow_dispatch:
16	    inputs:
17	      publish:
18	        description: 'Publish the desktop-v<version> Release (rebuilt in place; one version, no beta)'
19	        type: boolean
20	        default: true
21	  # Reusable so nightly-merge.yml can invoke the exact same build.
22	  workflow_call:
23	    inputs:
24	      publish:
25	        type: boolean
26	        default: false
27	      ref:
28	        description: 'Git ref to build (default: the triggering ref)'
29	        type: string
30	        default: ''
31	
32	permissions:
33	  contents: write
34	
35	jobs:
36	  build:
37	    runs-on: windows-11-arm
38	    steps:
39	      # Superset has paths deeper than Windows' 260-char MAX_PATH; without this
40	      # actions/checkout dies with "Filename too long". --system so the
41	      # checkout action's own git invocation picks it up.
42	      - name: Configure git long paths (Windows MAX_PATH)
43	        shell: bash
44	        run: git config --system core.longpaths true || git config --global core.longpaths true
45	
46	      - name: Checkout fork source
47	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
48	        with:
49	          ref: ${{ inputs.ref || github.ref }}
50	          fetch-depth: 1
51	
52	      - name: Read app version
53	        id: ver
54	        shell: bash
55	        run: |
56	          V=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
57	          [ -n "$V" ] || { echo "::error::could not read apps/desktop/package.json version"; exit 1; }
58	          echo "version=$V" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
59	          echo "App version: $V"
60	
61	      - name: Setup Node.js
62	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
63	        with:
64	          node-version: '22'
65	
66	      - name: Setup Bun (native Windows ARM64)
67	        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
68	        with:
69	          # 1.3.14 = last stable Zig release; first native win-arm64 shipped in 1.3.10.
70	          bun-version: '1.3.14'
71	
72	      - name: Verify Bun is native ARM64 (fail fast, fall back to official zip)
73	        shell: pwsh
74	        run: |
75	          function Get-PEMachine($p){ $fs=[IO.File]::OpenRead($p); $br=New-Object IO.BinaryReader($fs); $fs.Position=0x3C; $pe=$br.ReadInt32(); $fs.Position=$pe+4; $m=$br.ReadUInt16(); $br.Close(); return $m }
76	          $bun = (Get-Command bun).Source
77	          $m = Get-PEMachine $bun
78	          $ver = (& bun --version).Trim()
79	          Write-Host "bun: $bun  version=$ver  PEmachine=0x$('{0:X}' -f $m)"
80	          if ($m -ne 0xAA64) {
81	            Write-Host "::warning::setup-bun did not provide native ARM64 bun (machine 0x$('{0:X}' -f $m)); downloading official bun-windows-aarch64"
82	            $zip = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP 'bun-arm64.zip'
83	            Invoke-WebRequest -Uri 'https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/bun-v1.3.14/bun-windows-aarch64.zip' -OutFile $zip
84	            $dest = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP 'bun-arm64'
85	            Expand-Archive -Path $zip -DestinationPath $dest -Force
86	            $found = Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter bun.exe $dest | Select-Object -First 1
87	            if (-not $found) { Write-Error 'bun.exe not found in official ARM64 zip'; exit 1 }
88	            $m2 = Get-PEMachine $found.FullName
89	            if ($m2 -ne 0xAA64) { Write-Error "Official zip bun.exe is not ARM64 (0x$('{0:X}' -f $m2))"; exit 1 }
90	            Add-Content -Path $env:GITHUB_PATH -Value $found.DirectoryName
91	            Write-Host "Prepended native ARM64 bun ($($found.DirectoryName)) to PATH"
92	          }
93	
94	      - name: Install dependencies (frozen lockfile)
95	        shell: bash
96	        env:
97	          # Skip the flaky @electron/rebuild postinstall (install:deps). On
98	          # win-arm64 it can't build native trustedDeps; the deterministic
99	          # Bun-store payload repair (materialize-native-closure) handles them.
100	          SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING: '1'
101	        run: |
102	          set -euo pipefail
103	          # The committed bun.lock is the source of truth and is consistent with
104	          # our pinned package.json (node-pty @lydell alias, mastra pins), so a
105	          # FROZEN install uses the locked versions and never re-resolves — which
106	          # also sidesteps bunfig's minimumReleaseAge gate (locked versions are
107	          # not age-checked). If this fails the lock drifted from package.json:
108	          # regenerate it (bun install --lockfile-only) and recommit.
109	          bun install --frozen-lockfile
110	
111	      - name: Generate file icons
112	        working-directory: apps/desktop
113	        shell: bash
114	        run: bun run generate:icons
115	
116	      - name: Build app (electron-vite / esbuild)
117	        working-directory: apps/desktop
118	        shell: bash
119	        run: bun run compile:app
120	
121	      - name: Verify screenReaderMode stayed false in both renderer bundles
122	        working-directory: apps/desktop
123	        shell: bash
124	        run: |
125	          set -euo pipefail
126	          assets_dir="dist/renderer/assets"
127	          [ -d "$assets_dir" ] || { echo "::error::renderer assets dir not found at $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
128	          v1_chunk=$(ls -1 "$assets_dir"/config-*.js 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
129	          v2_chunk=$(ls -1 "$assets_dir"/terminal-runtime-*.js 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
130	          [ -n "$v1_chunk" ] || { echo "::error::v1 config-*.js chunk not found in $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
131	          [ -n "$v2_chunk" ] || { echo "::error::v2 terminal-runtime-*.js chunk not found in $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
132	          if grep -qE 'screenReaderMode[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*(true|!0)' "$v1_chunk"; then
133	            echo "::error::screenReaderMode TRUTHY in v1 chunk (Wispr Flow regression)"; exit 1
134	          fi
135	          if grep -qE 'screenReaderMode[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*(true|!0)' "$v2_chunk"; then
136	            echo "::error::screenReaderMode TRUTHY in v2 chunk (Wispr Flow regression)"; exit 1
137	          fi
138	          echo "screenReaderMode verified false in both renderer bundles."
139	
140	      - name: Fetch @libsql + @anush008/tokenizers win32-arm64 + materialize native closure
141	        shell: bash
142	        env:
143	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
144	        run: |
145	          set -euo pipefail
146	          LV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/libsql@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/libsql@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
147	          [ -n "$LV" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved libsql version"; exit 1; }
148	          echo "Resolved libsql: $LV"
149	          rm -rf libsql-dl libsql-arm64
150	          if ! gh release download "$LV" --repo khairm/libsql-windows-arm64 \
151	                 -p 'libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz' -p 'libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256' -D libsql-dl; then
152	            echo "::error::No @libsql/win32-arm64-msvc Release for libsql $LV in khairm/libsql-windows-arm64."
153	            echo "::error::Trigger that repo's nightly (workflow_dispatch -f libsql_version=$LV) then re-run."
154	            exit 1
155	          fi
156	          ( cd libsql-dl && sha256sum -c libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256 )
157	          mkdir -p libsql-arm64
158	          tar -xzf libsql-dl/libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz -C libsql-arm64
159	          [ -f libsql-arm64/index.node ] || { echo "::error::libsql artifact missing index.node"; exit 1; }
160	
161	          TV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/@anush008+tokenizers@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/@anush008+tokenizers@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
162	          [ -n "$TV" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved @anush008/tokenizers version"; exit 1; }
163	          echo "Resolved @anush008/tokenizers: $TV"
164	          rm -rf tok-dl tokenizers-arm64
165	          if ! gh release download "$TV" --repo khairm/tokenizers-windows-arm64 \
166	                 -p 'tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz' -p 'tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256' -D tok-dl; then
167	            echo "::error::No @anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc Release for tokenizers $TV in khairm/tokenizers-windows-arm64."
168	            echo "::error::Trigger that repo's nightly (workflow_dispatch -f tokenizers_version=$TV) then re-run."
169	            exit 1
170	          fi
171	          ( cd tok-dl && sha256sum -c tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256 )
172	          mkdir -p tokenizers-arm64
173	          tar -xzf tok-dl/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz -C tokenizers-arm64
174	          [ -f tokenizers-arm64/tokenizers.win32-arm64-msvc.node ] || { echo "::error::tokenizers artifact missing .node"; exit 1; }
175	
176	          # Electron NODE_MODULE_VERSION (V8 ABI). better-sqlite3 is V8-ABI-bound;
177	          # a wrong ABI fetches a prebuilt that crashes Electron. Derive
178	          # authoritatively via node-abi against the resolved electron, fall back
179	          # to a pinned map, HARD-FAIL on an unknown major (never silently guess).
180	          EM=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/electron@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/electron@##' | cut -d. -f1 | sort -n | tail -1)
181	          EV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/electron@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/electron@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
182	          [ -n "$EM" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved electron version"; exit 1; }
183	          ABI=$(node -e "try{process.stdout.write(String(require('node-abi').getAbi('$EV','electron')))}catch(e){}" 2>/dev/null || true)
184	          if printf '%s' "$ABI" | grep -Eq '^[0-9]+$'; then
185	            echo "ABI from node-abi getAbi('$EV','electron') = $ABI"
186	          else
187	            case "$EM" in
188	              36) ABI=135;; 37) ABI=136;; 38) ABI=139;; 39) ABI=140;; 40) ABI=143;; 41) ABI=145;; 42) ABI=146;;
189	              *) echo "::error::Unknown Electron major $EM and node-abi unavailable — add its NODE_MODULE_VERSION to the ABI map"; exit 1;;
190	            esac
191	            echo "ABI from pinned map (node-abi unavailable) = $ABI"
192	          fi
193	          echo "electron version=$EV major=$EM -> ABI=$ABI"
194	          export ELECTRON_ABI="$ABI" LIBSQL_ARM64_DIR="$PWD/libsql-arm64" TOKENIZERS_ARM64_DIR="$PWD/tokenizers-arm64"
195	          bash scripts/materialize-native-closure.sh
196	          echo "ELECTRON_ABI=$ABI" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
197	          echo "LIBSQL_ARM64_DIR=$PWD/libsql-arm64" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
198	          echo "TOKENIZERS_ARM64_DIR=$PWD/tokenizers-arm64" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
199	
200	      - name: Prepare native modules (ARM64 target)
201	        working-directory: apps/desktop
202	        shell: bash
203	        env:
204	          TARGET_ARCH: arm64
205	          TARGET_PLATFORM: win32
206	        run: bun run copy:native-modules
207	
208	      - name: Validate native runtime (ARM64 fail-fast gate)
209	        shell: bash
210	        env:
211	          TARGET_ARCH: arm64
212	          TARGET_PLATFORM: win32
213	        run: |
214	          set -euo pipefail
215	          bash scripts/materialize-native-closure.sh
216	          cd apps/desktop
217	          bun run validate:native-runtime
218	
219	      - name: Build Windows ARM64 installer
220	        working-directory: apps/desktop
221	        shell: bash
222	        env:
223	          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: 'false'
224	          # native-keymap etc. use V8 APIs Electron 40 deprecated; MSVC C4996
225	          # under warnings-as-errors fails node-gyp. /wd4996 disables just that
226	          # warning for all native rebuilds (deterministic; runtime is fine).
227	          CL: '/wd4996'
228	        run: |
229	          set -euo pipefail
230	          npx electron-builder --win --arm64 --publish never --config electron-builder.ts
231	          echo "--- release dir ---"
232	          ls -la release || true
233	
234	      - name: Verify packaged native closure (ARM64 fail-fast gate)
235	        working-directory: apps/desktop
236	        shell: bash
237	        run: |
238	          set -euo pipefail
239	          APP="release/win-arm64-unpacked"
240	          [ -d "$APP" ] || { echo "::error::$APP not found"; exit 1; }
241	          pearch() { od -An -tx1 -j"$(( $(od -An -tu4 -j60 -N4 "$1"|tr -d ' ')+4 ))" -N2 "$1" | tr -d ' '; }
242	          fail=0
243	          m="$(pearch "$APP/Superset.exe")"
244	          [ "$m" = 64aa ] && echo "OK  Superset.exe ARM64" || { echo "::error::Superset.exe not ARM64 (0x$m)"; fail=1; }
245	          TOK="$APP/resources/node_modules/@anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc/tokenizers.win32-arm64-msvc.node"
246	          if [ -f "$TOK" ] && [ "$(pearch "$TOK")" = 64aa ]; then
247	            echo "OK  @anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc packaged (ARM64)"
248	          else
249	            echo "::error::@anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc missing/!ARM64 at $TOK"; fail=1
250	          fi
251	          while IFS= read -r f; do
252	            case "$f" in
253	              *darwin*|*linux*|*musl*|*win32-ia32*|*win32-x64*|*win32_x64*|*win32_ia32*) continue ;;
254	            esac
255	            a="$(pearch "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
256	            if echo "$f" | grep -Eqi 'win32.?arm64|win32_arm64'; then
257	              [ "$a" = 64aa ] || { echo "::error::win32-arm64 native not ARM64: $f (0x$a)"; fail=1; }
258	            fi
259	          done < <(find "$APP/resources" -name '*.node' 2>/dev/null)
260	          [ "$fail" = 0 ] || { echo "::error::packaged native closure verification FAILED"; exit 1; }
261	          echo "Packaged native closure verified (ARM64)."
262	
263	      - name: Resolve installer asset
264	        id: asset
265	        if: inputs.publish
266	        shell: bash
267	        run: |
268	          set -euo pipefail
269	          A=$(ls apps/desktop/release/*arm64*.exe 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
270	          [ -n "$A" ] || A=$(ls apps/desktop/release/*.exe 2>/dev/null | grep -v blockmap | head -1 || true)
271	          [ -n "$A" ] || { echo "::error::no .exe produced in apps/desktop/release/"; ls -la apps/desktop/release || true; exit 1; }
272	          echo "path=$A" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
273	          echo "Installer: $A"
274	
275	      - name: Publish GitHub Release
276	        if: inputs.publish
277	        env:
278	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
279	        shell: bash
280	        run: |
281	          set -euo pipefail
282	          V="${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}"
283	          ASSET="${{ steps.asset.outputs.path }}"
284	          # ONE version per upstream release: desktop-v<version>, rebuilt in
285	          # place. Delete + recreate so the tag always points at the latest
286	          # build of that version. No beta / prerelease split.
287	          TAG="desktop-v${V}"
288	          gh release delete "$TAG" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" --yes 2>/dev/null || true
289	          git push --delete origin "refs/tags/$TAG" 2>/dev/null || true
290	          gh release create "$TAG" "$ASSET" \
291	            --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
292	            --title "Superset $V (Windows ARM64)" \
293	            --notes "Native **Windows ARM64** build of the vendored superset-sh/superset fork (version $V). Built deterministically from committed source (no AI in the build). Installer is **unsigned**. Not the official superset-sh distribution."
294

file.content:
name: Build Windows ARM64 (vendored fork)

# Deterministic, NO-AI build of the vendored fork source at repo root.
# Unlike the legacy pipeline there is NO upstream clone, NO patch apply, NO AI
# re-anchor and NO arch-fixup: every Windows/ARM64 change already lives in the
# committed source (the fork tree). This workflow only: install -> build ->
# materialize the win-arm64 native closure -> package the NSIS installer ->
# verify the packaged natives are ARM64 -> (optionally) publish a release.
#
# It is the build half of the architecture: the baseline is frozen in this repo;
# nightly-merge.yml carries upstream deltas in and calls a build like this to
# prove the result before publishing.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      publish:
        description: 'Publish the desktop-v<version> Release (rebuilt in place; one version, no beta)'
        type: boolean
        default: true
  # Reusable so nightly-merge.yml can invoke the exact same build.
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      publish:
        type: boolean
        default: false
      ref:
        description: 'Git ref to build (default: the triggering ref)'
        type: string
        default: ''

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: windows-11-arm
    steps:
      # Superset has paths deeper than Windows' 260-char MAX_PATH; without this
      # actions/checkout dies with "Filename too long". --system so the
      # checkout action's own git invocation picks it up.
      - name: Configure git long paths (Windows MAX_PATH)
        shell: bash
        run: git config --system core.longpaths true || git config --global core.longpaths true

      - name: Checkout fork source
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.ref || github.ref }}
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Read app version
        id: ver
        shell: bash
        run: |
          V=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          [ -n "$V" ] || { echo "::error::could not read apps/desktop/package.json version"; exit 1; }
          echo "version=$V" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "App version: $V"

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      - name: Setup Bun (native Windows ARM64)
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          # 1.3.14 = last stable Zig release; first native win-arm64 shipped in 1.3.10.
          bun-version: '1.3.14'

      - name: Verify Bun is native ARM64 (fail fast, fall back to official zip)
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          function Get-PEMachine($p){ $fs=[IO.File]::OpenRead($p); $br=New-Object IO.BinaryReader($fs); $fs.Position=0x3C; $pe=$br.ReadInt32(); $fs.Position=$pe+4; $m=$br.ReadUInt16(); $br.Close(); return $m }
          $bun = (Get-Command bun).Source
          $m = Get-PEMachine $bun
          $ver = (& bun --version).Trim()
          Write-Host "bun: $bun  version=$ver  PEmachine=0x$('{0:X}' -f $m)"
          if ($m -ne 0xAA64) {
            Write-Host "::warning::setup-bun did not provide native ARM64 bun (machine 0x$('{0:X}' -f $m)); downloading official bun-windows-aarch64"
            $zip = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP 'bun-arm64.zip'
            Invoke-WebRequest -Uri 'https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/releases/download/bun-v1.3.14/bun-windows-aarch64.zip' -OutFile $zip
            $dest = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP 'bun-arm64'
            Expand-Archive -Path $zip -DestinationPath $dest -Force
            $found = Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter bun.exe $dest | Select-Object -First 1
            if (-not $found) { Write-Error 'bun.exe not found in official ARM64 zip'; exit 1 }
            $m2 = Get-PEMachine $found.FullName
            if ($m2 -ne 0xAA64) { Write-Error "Official zip bun.exe is not ARM64 (0x$('{0:X}' -f $m2))"; exit 1 }
            Add-Content -Path $env:GITHUB_PATH -Value $found.DirectoryName
            Write-Host "Prepended native ARM64 bun ($($found.DirectoryName)) to PATH"
          }

      - name: Install dependencies (frozen lockfile)
        shell: bash
        env:
          # Skip the flaky @electron/rebuild postinstall (install:deps). On
          # win-arm64 it can't build native trustedDeps; the deterministic
          # Bun-store payload repair (materialize-native-closure) handles them.
          SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING: '1'
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # The committed bun.lock is the source of truth and is consistent with
          # our pinned package.json (node-pty @lydell alias, mastra pins), so a
          # FROZEN install uses the locked versions and never re-resolves — which
          # also sidesteps bunfig's minimumReleaseAge gate (locked versions are
          # not age-checked). If this fails the lock drifted from package.json:
          # regenerate it (bun install --lockfile-only) and recommit.
          bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Generate file icons
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        run: bun run generate:icons

      - name: Build app (electron-vite / esbuild)
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        run: bun run compile:app

      - name: Verify screenReaderMode stayed false in both renderer bundles
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          assets_dir="dist/renderer/assets"
          [ -d "$assets_dir" ] || { echo "::error::renderer assets dir not found at $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
          v1_chunk=$(ls -1 "$assets_dir"/config-*.js 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
          v2_chunk=$(ls -1 "$assets_dir"/terminal-runtime-*.js 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
          [ -n "$v1_chunk" ] || { echo "::error::v1 config-*.js chunk not found in $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
          [ -n "$v2_chunk" ] || { echo "::error::v2 terminal-runtime-*.js chunk not found in $assets_dir"; exit 1; }
          if grep -qE 'screenReaderMode[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*(true|!0)' "$v1_chunk"; then
            echo "::error::screenReaderMode TRUTHY in v1 chunk (Wispr Flow regression)"; exit 1
          fi
          if grep -qE 'screenReaderMode[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*(true|!0)' "$v2_chunk"; then
            echo "::error::screenReaderMode TRUTHY in v2 chunk (Wispr Flow regression)"; exit 1
          fi
          echo "screenReaderMode verified false in both renderer bundles."

      - name: Fetch @libsql + @anush008/tokenizers win32-arm64 + materialize native closure
        shell: bash
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          LV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/libsql@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/libsql@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
          [ -n "$LV" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved libsql version"; exit 1; }
          echo "Resolved libsql: $LV"
          rm -rf libsql-dl libsql-arm64
          if ! gh release download "$LV" --repo khairm/libsql-windows-arm64 \
                 -p 'libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz' -p 'libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256' -D libsql-dl; then
            echo "::error::No @libsql/win32-arm64-msvc Release for libsql $LV in khairm/libsql-windows-arm64."
            echo "::error::Trigger that repo's nightly (workflow_dispatch -f libsql_version=$LV) then re-run."
            exit 1
          fi
          ( cd libsql-dl && sha256sum -c libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256 )
          mkdir -p libsql-arm64
          tar -xzf libsql-dl/libsql-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz -C libsql-arm64
          [ -f libsql-arm64/index.node ] || { echo "::error::libsql artifact missing index.node"; exit 1; }

          TV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/@anush008+tokenizers@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/@anush008+tokenizers@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
          [ -n "$TV" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved @anush008/tokenizers version"; exit 1; }
          echo "Resolved @anush008/tokenizers: $TV"
          rm -rf tok-dl tokenizers-arm64
          if ! gh release download "$TV" --repo khairm/tokenizers-windows-arm64 \
                 -p 'tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz' -p 'tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256' -D tok-dl; then
            echo "::error::No @anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc Release for tokenizers $TV in khairm/tokenizers-windows-arm64."
            echo "::error::Trigger that repo's nightly (workflow_dispatch -f tokenizers_version=$TV) then re-run."
            exit 1
          fi
          ( cd tok-dl && sha256sum -c tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz.sha256 )
          mkdir -p tokenizers-arm64
          tar -xzf tok-dl/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc.tar.gz -C tokenizers-arm64
          [ -f tokenizers-arm64/tokenizers.win32-arm64-msvc.node ] || { echo "::error::tokenizers artifact missing .node"; exit 1; }

          # Electron NODE_MODULE_VERSION (V8 ABI). better-sqlite3 is V8-ABI-bound;
          # a wrong ABI fetches a prebuilt that crashes Electron. Derive
          # authoritatively via node-abi against the resolved electron, fall back
          # to a pinned map, HARD-FAIL on an unknown major (never silently guess).
          EM=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/electron@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/electron@##' | cut -d. -f1 | sort -n | tail -1)
          EV=$(ls -d node_modules/.bun/electron@* 2>/dev/null | sed 's#.*/electron@##' | sort -V | tail -1)
          [ -n "$EM" ] || { echo "::error::could not determine resolved electron version"; exit 1; }
          ABI=$(node -e "try{process.stdout.write(String(require('node-abi').getAbi('$EV','electron')))}catch(e){}" 2>/dev/null || true)
          if printf '%s' "$ABI" | grep -Eq '^[0-9]+$'; then
            echo "ABI from node-abi getAbi('$EV','electron') = $ABI"
          else
            case "$EM" in
              36) ABI=135;; 37) ABI=136;; 38) ABI=139;; 39) ABI=140;; 40) ABI=143;; 41) ABI=145;; 42) ABI=146;;
              *) echo "::error::Unknown Electron major $EM and node-abi unavailable — add its NODE_MODULE_VERSION to the ABI map"; exit 1;;
            esac
            echo "ABI from pinned map (node-abi unavailable) = $ABI"
          fi
          echo "electron version=$EV major=$EM -> ABI=$ABI"
          export ELECTRON_ABI="$ABI" LIBSQL_ARM64_DIR="$PWD/libsql-arm64" TOKENIZERS_ARM64_DIR="$PWD/tokenizers-arm64"
          bash scripts/materialize-native-closure.sh
          echo "ELECTRON_ABI=$ABI" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          echo "LIBSQL_ARM64_DIR=$PWD/libsql-arm64" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          echo "TOKENIZERS_ARM64_DIR=$PWD/tokenizers-arm64" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Prepare native modules (ARM64 target)
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        env:
          TARGET_ARCH: arm64
          TARGET_PLATFORM: win32
        run: bun run copy:native-modules

      - name: Validate native runtime (ARM64 fail-fast gate)
        shell: bash
        env:
          TARGET_ARCH: arm64
          TARGET_PLATFORM: win32
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          bash scripts/materialize-native-closure.sh
          cd apps/desktop
          bun run validate:native-runtime

      - name: Build Windows ARM64 installer
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        env:
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: 'false'
          # native-keymap etc. use V8 APIs Electron 40 deprecated; MSVC C4996
          # under warnings-as-errors fails node-gyp. /wd4996 disables just that
          # warning for all native rebuilds (deterministic; runtime is fine).
          CL: '/wd4996'
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          npx electron-builder --win --arm64 --publish never --config electron-builder.ts
          echo "--- release dir ---"
          ls -la release || true

      - name: Verify packaged native closure (ARM64 fail-fast gate)
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          APP="release/win-arm64-unpacked"
          [ -d "$APP" ] || { echo "::error::$APP not found"; exit 1; }
          pearch() { od -An -tx1 -j"$(( $(od -An -tu4 -j60 -N4 "$1"|tr -d ' ')+4 ))" -N2 "$1" | tr -d ' '; }
          fail=0
          m="$(pearch "$APP/Superset.exe")"
          [ "$m" = 64aa ] && echo "OK  Superset.exe ARM64" || { echo "::error::Superset.exe not ARM64 (0x$m)"; fail=1; }
          TOK="$APP/resources/node_modules/@anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc/tokenizers.win32-arm64-msvc.node"
          if [ -f "$TOK" ] && [ "$(pearch "$TOK")" = 64aa ]; then
            echo "OK  @anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc packaged (ARM64)"
          else
            echo "::error::@anush008/tokenizers-win32-arm64-msvc missing/!ARM64 at $TOK"; fail=1
          fi
          while IFS= read -r f; do
            case "$f" in
              *darwin*|*linux*|*musl*|*win32-ia32*|*win32-x64*|*win32_x64*|*win32_ia32*) continue ;;
            esac
            a="$(pearch "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            if echo "$f" | grep -Eqi 'win32.?arm64|win32_arm64'; then
              [ "$a" = 64aa ] || { echo "::error::win32-arm64 native not ARM64: $f (0x$a)"; fail=1; }
            fi
          done < <(find "$APP/resources" -name '*.node' 2>/dev/null)
          [ "$fail" = 0 ] || { echo "::error::packaged native closure verification FAILED"; exit 1; }
          echo "Packaged native closure verified (ARM64)."

      - name: Resolve installer asset
        id: asset
        if: inputs.publish
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          A=$(ls apps/desktop/release/*arm64*.exe 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
          [ -n "$A" ] || A=$(ls apps/desktop/release/*.exe 2>/dev/null | grep -v blockmap | head -1 || true)
          [ -n "$A" ] || { echo "::error::no .exe produced in apps/desktop/release/"; ls -la apps/desktop/release || true; exit 1; }
          echo "path=$A" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Installer: $A"

      - name: Publish GitHub Release
        if: inputs.publish
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          V="${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}"
          ASSET="${{ steps.asset.outputs.path }}"
          # ONE version per upstream release: desktop-v<version>, rebuilt in
          # place. Delete + recreate so the tag always points at the latest
          # build of that version. No beta / prerelease split.
          TAG="desktop-v${V}"
          gh release delete "$TAG" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" --yes 2>/dev/null || true
          git push --delete origin "refs/tags/$TAG" 2>/dev/null || true
          gh release create "$TAG" "$ASSET" \
            --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
            --title "Superset $V (Windows ARM64)" \
            --notes "Native **Windows ARM64** build of the vendored superset-sh/superset fork (version $V). Built deterministically from committed source (no AI in the build). Installer is **unsigned**. Not the official superset-sh distribution."

content.content:
1	name: Nightly upstream merge
2	
3	# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
4	# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
5	# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
6	# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
7	# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
8	# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
9	# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
10	# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
11	#
12	# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
13	# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
14	#
15	# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
16	# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
17	
18	on:
19	  schedule:
20	    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
21	  workflow_dispatch:
22	    inputs:
23	      force_tag:
24	        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
25	        required: false
26	        default: ''
27	
28	permissions:
29	  contents: write
30	
31	jobs:
32	  merge:
33	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
34	    outputs:
35	      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
36	      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
37	      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
38	      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
39	    env:
40	      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

100	          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------

102	          git switch -c "$CAND"
103	          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
104	          set +e

106	          set -e

Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. 116	              echo "::endgroup::"
117	              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
118	              attempt=$((attempt+1))
119	            done

121	            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
122	              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"

129	          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------

152	          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
153	          # runner spins up), not here:

155	          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
156	          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
157	          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
158	          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
159	          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
160	          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).

162	          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,

167	          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
168	          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
169	          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
170	          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
171	          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"

173	          echo "::endgroup::"
174	
175	          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
176	          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
177	          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
178	          set +e
179	          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
180	          rc=$?
181	          set -e
182	          cat "$LOCKLOG"
183	          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
184	            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
185	              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
186	            else
187	              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
188	            fi
189	            exit 1
190	          fi
191	          echo "::endgroup::"
192	
193	          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
194	          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
195	          git add -A
196	          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."

198	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
199	          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
200	          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
201	          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
202	          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
203	          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
204	          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
205	          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
206	          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
207	          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
208	          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
209	          git push origin "$CAND"
210	
211	          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
212	          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
213	          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
214	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
215	          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
216	          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND (version $VERSION). Building next."
217	
218	  build:
219	    needs: merge
220	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
221	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
222	    with:
223	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}
224	      publish: true
225	
226	  advance:
227	    needs: [merge, build]
228	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
229	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
230	    steps:
231	      - name: Checkout
232	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
233	        with:
234	          fetch-depth: 0
235	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
236	        shell: bash
237	        run: |
238	          set -euo pipefail
239	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
240	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
241	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
242	          git push origin FETCH_HEAD:refs/heads/main
243	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
244	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

file.content:
name: Nightly upstream merge

# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
#
# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
#
# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      force_tag:
        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
        required: false
        default: ''

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
    env:
      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
      # frozen build install consumes.
      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: '1.3.14'

      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
        id: do
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"

          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
          FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'
          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
          else
            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
          fi
          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
          fi

          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
          git switch -c "$CAND"
          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
          set +e
          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
          set -e
          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          MISSING=0
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin FETCH_HEAD:refs/heads/main
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

lines: | Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |

| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |

| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |

+| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |

| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |

file.content:
`desktop-v<version>` Release** (rebuilt in place — see One-version below) and
  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker, build
  failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline untouched → recovery.
- **Recovery.** When a nightly merge can't be done cleanly, fix it locally with the
  maintainer, rebuild, re-freeze the baseline; the nightly then resumes merging only
  *new* upstream changes from that point.

## Fork non-negotiables

- **Whole feature set or fail loud.** Every `FEATURES.md` marker survives a merge or
  the build aborts. Never ship a partial fork.
- **v2-only, forever.** The v2 cloud/host-service stack is pinned on; never v1.
- **No build-time type/test gate.** The build runs electron-vite/esbuild only —
  type/format errors don't fail it. Validate + e2e locally before relying on a release.
- **AI only in the nightly merge.** The build is AI-free. The nightly conflict
  resolver needs `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`; if rate-limited it aborts rather than ship
  a half-merged fork.
- **One version, ever.** Exactly one GitHub Release per upstream version, tagged
  `desktop-v<version>`, **rebuilt in place** (delete + recreate so the tag always
  points at the latest build). NO `-beta`, NO prerelease, NO separate release/beta
  split — there is just *the* `desktop-v<version>` build.

## Fork features (high-level + UX, for a full rebuild)

See `FEATURES.md` for the marker manifest. In brief:

- **Native Windows ARM64 packaging** — one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty,
  libsql, tokenizers; renderer CORS for `superset-app://`.
- **Window controls** — native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on
  Windows, theme-matched; upstream's duplicate controls hidden on Windows.
- **Windows behaviour fixes** — skip quit-confirmation; cmd.exe fallback;
  force-foreground; hidden-window watchdog; WebGL first-paint recovery; Wispr Flow
  accessibility + paste fix (UIA reachable WITHOUT xterm screenReaderMode); fast
  non-blocking startup (no main-thread fs).
- **Agent status dots (Claude + Codex)** — a coloured dot per terminal + a workspace
  rollup: red = needs input, yellow = working (held while subagents run; also while
  Claude compacts context — PreCompact/SessionStart(source=compact) bracket it — and
  while agent-type background_tasks (teammates/forks/workflows) outlive the turn,
  and while a pid-alive codex-companion job for the session runs — held even
  through a Claude StopFailure, since codex is on its own API), green = ready
  for review, blue = a foreground shell command or a shell-only background
  remainder / cloud session (a manual /compact restores this blue from a
  turn-end snapshot marker instead of false-greening).
  Precedence red > yellow > blue > green. Host-service lifecycle POSTs + a JSONL
  watcher fallback; only open tabs are represented. superset-notify.py exclusively
  owns Claude's Stop — upstream's notify.sh is deliberately NOT registered on Stop
  (its raw passthrough raced ~1s behind and wiped BackgroundRunning blue + the
  subagent yellow-hold). Every dot surface (tab, pane header, sidebar row,
  workspace rollup, kanban card) derives from ONE per-source primitive in the
  v2-notifications store; the rollup is the fold of the per-source dots, so
  surfaces cannot drift. The primitive itself is layered (DOT-AXES): a source's
  status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission/working/
  review, plus the separate shell/background blue axes) — events latch/unlatch
  only the axes they have evidence about, so an assert can never stomp a
  higher-ranking active state; a tool that ran inside a subagent (hook payload
  carries agent_id, SUBTOOL-RED) asserts working WITHOUT clearing a pending
  red, while a main-loop tool completion still clears it (the answer proof).
  Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal (MARKER-RECONCILE): a SubagentStop
  arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id strands its run-dir marker and
  would pin yellow forever; every Stop/SubagentStop now reaps markers not

message.content:
Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="review-layout-fix" color="blue" summary="hold-gap fix verified sound, all 5 points pass">
Verified. All 5 points check out — this is a correct, surgical fix. No blocking findings.

**(1) Behavior-identical when the new clause is false — confirmed.** Algebraically: old `heldWorkspace = workspace ?? (isTransient && workspaceId && ref.id===workspaceId ? ref : null)` with `isTransient=!isReady`. New: `heldCandidate = (workspaceId && ref.id===workspaceId) ? ref : null`, `isTransient = !isReady || (...)`, `heldWorkspace = workspace ?? (isTransient ? heldCandidate : null)`. When the `resolveHostUrl` disjunct is false, `isTransient` reduces to `!isReady` exactly as before, and `isTransient ? heldCandidate : null` collapses to the same three-way AND the old inline expression used (heldCandidate is already null whenever the workspaceId/ref-match conjunct fails). Verified by cases, not just inspection — they're equivalent.

**(2) `hostId` id-space — confirmed correct.** Traced `packages/db/src/schema/relations.ts:333-336`: `v2Workspaces.hostId` is an explicit composite FK to `v2Hosts.machineId` (`[organizationId, hostId] → [organizationId, machineId]`). And `useHostWorkspaces.utils.ts` `applyWorkspaceChangedEvent` sets `hostId: host.machineId` directly. `cache.resolveHostUrl`/`targetFor` key on `target.machineId === hostId`. So `heldCandidate.hostId` is unambiguously in the same space `resolveHostUrl` expects — no mismatch.

**(3) No render-loop/exhaustive-deps issue — confirmed.** `heldCandidate`/`isTransient`/`heldWorkspace` are plain consts recomputed each render, same pattern as before (not new hooks). `cache` itself is memoized in `useHostWorkspacesSource` (`useMemo(..., [targets, queryClient])`), and `resolveHostUrl` is a pure lookup (`targets.find`) — safe to call inline during render. The `blankDbg` `useEffect`'s dependency array wasn't touched and doesn't reference `cache`, so no missing-dep gap. One minor (non-blocking) inefficiency: `cache.resolveHostUrl(...)` runs on *every* render even in the common steady "ready" case where `workspace` is non-null and the result is irrelevant (short-circuiting `workspace ?? ...` doesn't skip computing `isTransient` itself, since it's a separate statement) — cheap (`Array.find` over a handful of hosts), not worth blocking on.

**(4) Local host briefly down → hold, not not-found — confirmed desirable and correctly implemented, including the harder edge case.** When `activeHostUrl` is null (local host-service down) but the local machine still has a `v2Hosts` row, `deriveHostWorkspacesQueryTargets` still produces a target for it with `hostUrl: null` → `resolveHostUrl` returns null → hold. Checked the harder edge case too: if the local host's `v2Hosts` row is *also* missing (fresh install / stale Electric) at the same time `activeHostUrl` is null, the synthetic local-target fallback (which requires `activeHostUrl` truthy) doesn't fire either — but then `targetFor` finds nothing at all and `resolveHostUrl` still returns null via `?? null`. Same outcome: hold. No path where local-down produces a false not-found.

**(5) Not-found still reachable for a genuine delete on a reachable host — confirmed.** Walked the case: `workspace` null (real delete), `isReady` true, `heldCandidate` = the stale ref, but `resolveHostUrl(heldCandidate.hostId)` returns a real URL (host reachable) → second disjunct false → `isTransient` false → `heldWorkspace = null` → `renderBranch` = "not-found". This is exactly the scoping I'd have wanted: the hold only engages when the *specific remembered workspace's own host* is unreachable, not merely "some row disappeared while some host is offline."

**One design note, not a bug:** the hold has no time bound. If a remote host is offline *permanently* (not transiently — e.g. the machine is gone for good), `resolveHostUrl` stays null forever and the open workspace holds its last-known content indefinitely rather than ever falling through to not-found. `useRemoteHostStatus` also doesn't have an "offline" status — it unconditionally returns `"ready"` once `workspace` is non-null and not local (no gate on `infoQuery` pending/error), so the held workspace fully mounts `WorkspaceProvider`/`Outlet` against an unreachable host. This matches the existing cache-first-hold philosophy (favor stale-but-visible over a false not-found, per AGENTS.md rule 9) and isn't something this diff introduced, but flagging so it's a known, intentional trade-off if it ever comes up ("why does a long-gone remote workspace still show old content instead of not-found").

Verdict: ship it.
</teammate-message>

accepted type debt contains zero of these codes, so the gate cannot
false-abort. Wired into build-arm64.yml after install — covers manual
builds AND every nightly (its build job calls the same workflow).
Proven locally: 49s, 7 packages, 0 dangerous / 40 debt diagnostics
allowed.

EOF

content.text:
All shipped. Final build `29110546981` running on `26e461fa9` — it's also the first build to run the new gate, so the gate gets validated live in CI. Summary of this incident batch:

- **Crash** (`ReferenceError: workspaces is not defined`): the v1.14.0 nightly merge renamed the workspace source in a hunk Git merged cleanly, so the AI resolver never saw the fork's cache-first-hold lines still using the old name. Latent in the 1.14.0 and 1.14.2 exes. Fixed in `719cf3c74`; two independent reviewers + a 7-package sweep confirm it was the only break of its class.
- **Hold gap** (your "fix now"): open workspace now holds instead of flashing not-found when its host is unreachable (`335819150`, reviewer-verified on all 5 risk points).
- **REFERR-GATE** (your "nightly + build"): `26e461fa9` adds a per-package tsc gate failing only on cannot-find-name codes — the class that caused this crash — wired into `build-arm64.yml`, which both manual builds and the nightly's build job use. Proven locally: 49 s, 0 dangerous, all known type debt still allowed. The merge job itself stays install-free (its documented linux false-abort concern), coverage comes via its build job.
- Superseded crash-fix-only build cancelled; the `desktop-v1.14.2` release will be rebuilt in place by the final build (~20-25 min). I'll ask for your install go when it's green — your currently installed build keeps crashing on workspace views until then.
